metadata.dispatch_line: Domestic disturbance reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, in the city of Lakewood, Colorado, with a female screaming for help

scenario.dispatch_line: Domestic disturbance reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, in the city of Lakewood, Colorado, with a female screaming for help

facts.domestic_violence_01: Yes, the individual, Jamal Jenkins, has a prior conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence
facts.domestic_violence_02: Yes, there is a qualifying order of protection in place against Jamal Jenkins, issued by the circuit court of Montgomery County, Maryland, on February 10, 2023
facts.domestic_violence_03: Yes, the report includes all needed information, such as the relationship between the parties, history of the relationship, visible injuries, complaints of pain, and signs and symptoms of strangulation
facts.domestic_violence_04: Yes, Officer Maria Rodriguez assisted the victim, Leila Patel, by providing information about obtaining an order of protection, local service providers, and utilizing a threat and risk assessment tool
facts.domestic_violence_05: The case was received as a priority response intimate partner violence call at 9:45 PM on March 12, 2023, from 1457 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, Lakewood, Colorado
facts.domestic_violence_06: Yes, information was provided to Leila Patel about obtaining an order of protection, including the phone number for the local domestic violence hotline and the address of the nearest shelter
facts.domestic_violence_07: Yes, the time of the call is recorded as 9:45 PM on March 12, 2023, with dispatch at 9:50 PM, and Officer Maria Rodriguez arriving at the scene at 10:05 PM
facts.domestic_violence_08: Yes, information was provided to Leila Patel about local service providers, including the phone number for the National Domestic Violence Hotline and the address of the nearest counseling center
facts.domestic_violence_09: Yes, the elements of the crime(s) are articulated to meet state and/or federal laws that address domestic violence, including Colorado's domestic violence statute and the federal Violence Against Women Act
facts.domestic_violence_10: Yes, a threat/risk assessment tool was used with Leila Patel to assess the level of danger and provide appropriate resources
facts.domestic_violence_11: Upon approach, Officer Maria Rodriguez observed that the front door was slightly ajar, and Leila Patel was standing in the foyer with visible bruises on her face and neck
facts.domestic_violence_12: Yes, there is a valid protection order in place, issued by the circuit court of Montgomery County, Maryland, on February 10, 2023, which prohibits Jamal Jenkins from contacting or approaching Leila Patel
facts.domestic_violence_13: Yes, the scene is concisely described and diagramed in the report, including the location of the injuries, the position of the parties, and the presence of any witnesses
facts.domestic_violence_14: Yes, photos were taken and details recorded, including photos of Leila Patel's injuries and the damage to the property
facts.domestic_violence_15: The relationship of the parties is identified as intimate partners, with Leila Patel and Jamal Jenkins having been in a romantic relationship for approximately two years
facts.domestic_violence_16: The history of the relationship includes frequent incidents of verbal and physical abuse, with Leila Patel reporting that Jamal Jenkins has a history of using intimidation and threats to control her
facts.domestic_violence_17: Yes, all witnesses were interviewed and documented, including Leila Patel's neighbor, who reported hearing loud arguing and screaming coming from the residence earlier that evening
facts.domestic_violence_18: Yes, an arrest was made, with Jamal Jenkins being taken into custody for violating the protection order and committing domestic violence
facts.domestic_violence_19: No, there were no children on the scene, as Leila Patel and Jamal Jenkins do not have any children together
facts.domestic_violence_20: Yes, information about previous incidents was documented, including a prior incident of domestic violence that occurred on January 15, 2023, which resulted in Leila Patel obtaining the protection order
facts.domestic_violence_21: Yes, a weapon/object was used, with Leila Patel reporting that Jamal Jenkins used a kitchen knife to threaten her during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_22: The emotional state of the victim, Leila Patel, was one of fear and anxiety, with her reporting that she was terrified of Jamal Jenkins and felt trapped in the relationship
facts.domestic_violence_23: The emotional state of the suspect, Jamal Jenkins, was one of agitation and hostility, with him becoming increasingly agitated during the arrest process
facts.domestic_violence_24: Evidence collected includes photos of Leila Patel's injuries, a kitchen knife used to threaten her, and a written statement from Leila Patel detailing the incident
facts.domestic_violence_25: No, a threat/risk assessment tool was not used with the suspect, Jamal Jenkins, as he was arrested and taken into custody immediately
facts.domestic_violence_26: Yes, evidence of fear is articulated in the report, with Leila Patel stating that she was terrified of Jamal Jenkins and felt trapped in the relationship
facts.domestic_violence_27: Yes, all threats made by Jamal Jenkins have been clearly documented, including his threat to harm Leila Patel with a kitchen knife
facts.domestic_violence_28: Yes, the proper protocol was followed, as Jamal Jenkins was present at the scene and was arrested
facts.domestic_violence_29: Yes, the use of coercion and/or force is articulated in the report, with Leila Patel reporting that Jamal Jenkins used physical force to intimidate and control her
facts.domestic_violence_30: Yes, all injuries, both visible and non-visible, have been documented, including Leila Patel's bruised face and neck, as well as her complaint of difficulty breathing
facts.domestic_violence_31: The injuries were a combination of existing and new, with Leila Patel reporting that some of her injuries were from previous incidents, while others were from the current incident
facts.domestic_violence_32: Yes, there was property damage, with Leila Patel reporting that Jamal Jenkins had damaged her phone and laptop during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_33: Yes, stalking behaviors have been identified, with Leila Patel reporting that Jamal Jenkins had been monitoring her movements and showing up at her workplace uninvited
facts.domestic_violence_34: Yes, the officer did inquire about possible strangulation, and Leila Patel reported that Jamal Jenkins had attempted to strangle her during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_35: Yes, the victim, Leila Patel, reported being strangled, with her stating that Jamal Jenkins had used his hands to constrict her neck
facts.domestic_violence_36: Yes, the victim requested medical attention, and EMS was summoned to the scene to treat her injuries
facts.domestic_violence_37: No, the victim did not report sexual violence during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_38: Yes, all spontaneous statements were captured, including Leila Patel's statement that Jamal Jenkins had threatened to kill her if she left him
facts.domestic_violence_39: Yes, the officer did ask about firearms, and Leila Patel reported that Jamal Jenkins had access to a handgun at his residence, located at 2345 Cedar Drive, Silver Spring, Maryland 20910
facts.domestic_violence_40: No, the suspect did not use firearms during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_41: Yes, the suspect made threats to use firearms, with Leila Patel reporting that Jamal Jenkins had threatened to shoot her if she called the police
facts.domestic_violence_42: Yes, the suspect has access to firearms, with Leila Patel reporting that Jamal Jenkins has a handgun at his residence
facts.domestic_violence_43: Yes, the victim expressed fear about the suspect's access to firearms, with Leila Patel stating that she was terrified that Jamal Jenkins would use the handgun to harm her
facts.domestic_violence_44: No, firearms were not confiscated at the scene, but a note was made in the report to seek a search warrant to confiscate the handgun from Jamal Jenkins' residence, located at 2345 Cedar Drive, Silver Spring, Maryland 20910

interview_transcript.content: Good afternoon. My name is Officer Johnson. I'm here to help you. Can you please state your name and date of birth for my records?
Good afternoon, Officer Johnson. My name is Leila Rashid Patel, and my date of birth is... (pausing for a moment) ...February 12, 1995.
Thank you, Ms. Patel. Can you tell me what your current address is, and how long you have lived there?

My current address is... (nervously) ...1457 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, in Lakewood, Colorado. I've lived there for about... (thinking) ...six months now. I moved in with my... (pausing, choosing her words carefully) ...with Jamal, my partner, at the time.
I understand. Can you tell me Jamal's full name, his date of birth, and his relationship to you?

(nervously) Yes... Jamal's full name is Jamal Jenkins. I'm not really sure of his date of birth, to be honest. (pausing, looking uncomfortable) As for our relationship... we've been in a romantic relationship for about two years now. (her voice cracks slightly) We've been having some problems, though. He's been... (trailing off, struggling to continue)
Take your time, Ms. Patel. I'm here to listen. Can you tell me what happened today? Please describe the incident from the beginning.

(taking a deep breath) Okay... It started earlier tonight. Jamal and I were arguing. He's been getting more and more controlling lately, and I just felt so trapped. (her voice starts to shake) We were in the living room, and he just started yelling at me. He said I was worthless, that I'd never leave him... (pausing, collecting her thoughts) And then he grabbed me. He grabbed my neck and... (touching her neck, wincing) He tried to strangle me. I thought he was going to... (tearfully) I thought he was going to kill me.

(composing herself slightly) I managed to get away from him, and I ran to the foyer. That's when I called the police. I was so scared. He was still yelling, still threatening me. (her eyes well up with tears) I just wanted it to stop. I wanted him to leave me alone.

(looking down, her voice barely above a whisper) I'm so sorry. I feel like this is all my fault. I should have left him sooner... (trailing off, overcome with emotion)
This is not your fault at all, Ms. Patel. You did the right thing by calling us. Are you injured? Do you have any visible marks or pain on your neck or anywhere else on your body?

(sniffling) Yes... I'm hurt. My neck is sore, and I have bruises on my face and neck. (touching her face and neck) He hit me and choked me. I also have some pain in my throat and chest. It's hard to breathe sometimes. (coughing slightly) And my head is spinning a bit.

(looking up, showing her injuries) You can see the bruises on my face, I think. (pointing to her neck and face) They're pretty visible. I'm also feeling a bit dizzy and lightheaded. (swaying slightly) I don't know if it's from the choking or just from being so scared... (tearfully) I just feel so overwhelmed.

(taking a deep breath) I think I might need some medical attention. I'm not sure. I just know I don't feel right. (looking at Officer Johnson with a pleading expression) Can I get some help? Please?
We will absolutely get you medical help. Do you know where Jamal is right now? Is he still inside the apartment or did he leave?

(shaking her head) I don't know. He was still here when I called the police, but I don't know if he's still inside or if he left. (looking around nervously) I didn't see him leave, but I was hiding in the foyer, waiting for you to arrive. (whispering) I was too scared to move.

(looking at Officer Johnson with a frightened expression) Do you think he's still here? Is he going to come back? (her voice trembling) I don't want him to hurt me again.
We're going to make sure you're safe. Did Jamal use any weapons during the assault, or did he threaten you with any weapon? Also, was anyone else present in the apartment tonight?

(shuddering at the memory) He used a kitchen knife. He held it up to my throat and said he would kill me if I tried to leave him. (covering her mouth, as if to stifle a scream) He said he would cut me, that he would make me pay for trying to leave him.

(shaking her head) No, there was no one else here tonight. We were alone in the apartment. (pausing, thinking) But I think my neighbor might have heard us arguing. I'm not sure, but I heard some noise from next door earlier. Maybe they called the police? (looking at Officer Johnson hopefully) Do you think they might have seen or heard something?

(her eyes widening in fear) Oh, God, what if he comes back? What if he tries to hurt me again? (grabbing Officer Johnson's arm) Please, you have to protect me. You have to keep me safe from him.
You are safe now. Has Jamal been physically violent with you before today? And do you have any existing restraining orders or protection orders against him?

(nodding, tears streaming down her face) Yes, he's been violent with me before. It's been getting worse and worse over time. He's pushed me, slapped me, and even choked me before. (touching her neck again, wincing at the memory) I've tried to leave him, but he always finds ways to manipulate me into staying.

(sniffling) Yes, I do have a protection order against him. I got it after the last time he hurt me, about a month ago. (pulling out a piece of paper from her pocket) I have it right here. It's from the circuit court of Montgomery County, Maryland. (handing the paper to Officer Johnson) It says he's not supposed to contact me or come near me, but... (shaking her head) He just doesn't care. He says he loves me, but he doesn't care about hurting me.

(looking down, her voice barely above a whisper) I'm so scared of him. I don't know what he's capable of. (glancing up at Officer Johnson) Do you think he'll go to jail this time? Do you think he'll finally leave me alone?

Thank you for sharing that. Can you tell me approximately what time tonight the argument and assault started? And can you describe Jamal for me - his approximate age, height, weight, and what he was wearing?

(thinking back) I think the argument started around... 9:30 or 9:40 pm. It's all a bit of a blur, but I remember looking at the clock on my phone and seeing that it was around 9:30. And then things just escalated from there.

(taking a deep breath) As for Jamal, he's... (pausing, trying to estimate) I think he's around 30 years old. He's about 5'10" or 5'11" tall, and he's pretty muscular. He weighs around 180 or 190 pounds, I think. (shuddering) He's a big guy, and he uses his size to intimidate me.

(as for what he was wearing) I think he was wearing a black t-shirt and some dark jeans. He might have had some sneakers on, too. (trying to remember) His hair is short and buzzed, and he has a bit of a scruff on his face. (looking up at Officer Johnson) I don't know if that helps, but... (shrugging) I just want him to leave me alone.

(looking down, her voice barely above a whisper) I'm so tired of being scared of him. I just want to feel safe again.
Can you tell me briefly what the argument was about that led to this assault? What was the initial cause of the dispute tonight?

(sighing) It was about me trying to leave him. Again. I've been trying to get out of the relationship for a while now, but he just won't let me go. Tonight, I told him that I'd had enough, that I was done with the abuse and the control. I said I was leaving him, and that I was going to go stay with a friend.

(shaking her head) He just lost it. He started yelling at me, saying that I'd never leave him, that I'd never be able to make it on my own. He said that I was nothing without him, that I'd never find anyone else who would put up with me. (looking up, her eyes filled with tears) And then he started hitting me, and... (trailing off, unable to continue)

narrative:
INCIDENT REPORT — DOMESTIC VIOLENCE / ASSAULT WITH A DEADLY WEAPON / VIOLATION OF PROTECTION ORDER

Reporting Officer: Officer Johnson
Date of Report: 04/28/2026
Incident Date: 04/28/2026
Incident Time: Approximately 2130–2140 hours
Location of Incident: 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B, Lakewood, Colorado
Case Type: Domestic Violence / Felony Menacing / Strangulation / Violation of Protection Order

PARTIES INVOLVED

Victim: Leila Rashid Patel
DOB: February 12, 1995
Address: 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apt. 3B, Lakewood, Colorado
Relationship to Suspect: Romantic partner (approximately 2-year relationship)

Suspect: Jamal Jenkins
DOB: Unknown
Address: 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apt. 3B, Lakewood, Colorado (co-resident)
Description: Black male, approximately 30 years of age, 5'10"–5'11" tall, approximately 180–190 lbs, muscular build, short buzzed hair, facial scruff. Last seen wearing a black t-shirt, dark jeans, and sneakers. Whereabouts at time of report unknown.

DISPATCH INFORMATION

Officers were dispatched to 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B, Lakewood, Colorado, in response to a domestic disturbance call reporting a female screaming for help.

NARRATIVE

Upon arrival, this officer made contact with the victim, Leila Rashid Patel, DOB 02/12/1995, in the foyer area of the apartment. The victim appeared visibly distressed, was crying, and exhibited visible injuries including bruising to her face and neck. The victim was shaking and expressed fear for her safety.

The victim stated that she has resided at the above address for approximately six months, having moved in with suspect Jamal Jenkins at that time. The victim described the relationship as a two-year romantic partnership, which she characterized as increasingly controlling and abusive.

According to the victim, at approximately 2130 hours on this date, she informed the suspect that she intended to leave the relationship and go stay with a friend. The victim stated that upon hearing this, the suspect became enraged, began yelling at her, and made derogatory statements including calling her "worthless" and stating she would "never leave him." The victim stated the suspect then physically attacked her in the living room of the apartment.

The victim reported that the suspect grabbed her by the neck with both hands and attempted to strangle her. She stated she believed the suspect was going to kill her. Additionally, the victim stated that the suspect retrieved a kitchen knife during the altercation, held it to her throat, and verbally threatened to kill her if she attempted to leave. The victim stated the suspect said he "would cut her" and "make her pay" for trying to leave him.

The victim stated she managed to break free from the suspect and fled to the foyer area of the apartment, where she called 911. At the time she placed the call, the suspect was reportedly still inside the apartment yelling and making threats. The victim stated she remained in the foyer until officers arrived. The suspect's current whereabouts were unknown upon officer arrival.

INJURIES

The victim reported the following injuries sustained during the assault: soreness and visible bruising to the neck and face; throat pain; chest pain; difficulty breathing; dizziness; and lightheadedness. The victim stated she was unsure whether her symptoms resulted from the strangulation or from extreme emotional distress. The victim requested medical attention. EMS notification was made.

PRIOR HISTORY AND PROTECTION ORDER

The victim disclosed a history of physical abuse by the suspect, describing previous incidents of pushing, slapping, and choking over the course of the relationship. The victim produced a civil Protection Order issued by the Circuit Court of Montgomery County, Maryland, approximately one month prior to this incident. The Protection Order prohibits the suspect from contacting or approaching the victim. The suspect's presence at the shared residence and commission of this assault constitutes a violation of the active Protection Order.

WITNESS INFORMATION

No other individuals were present in the apartment at the time of the incident. The victim indicated that a neighbor in the adjacent unit may have heard the argument, as noise was audible through the shared wall prior to the assault. Neighbor contact to be conducted as follow-up.

EVIDENCE NOTED

Active civil Protection Order (originating jurisdiction: Montgomery County, Maryland) — presented by victim at scene. Kitchen knife reported as weapon used; location of knife at time of report unknown. Injuries on victim consistent with described assault documented for photographic evidence.

DISPOSITION

This officer documented the victim's injuries for evidentiary purposes. EMS was requested for the victim. An attempt to locate the suspect was initiated. This report is forwarded for review and potential charges including: Domestic Violence Assault, Felony Menacing, Attempted Strangulation, and Violation of a Protection Order.
